name: pfd_ranges_no_gap
reason: If consecutive bins have no gap between pfd_upper(i-1) and pfd_lower(i), the partition is tight — but since size_of_bin is external_body, this tightness is only as trustworthy as the unverified constants lemma

name: size_of_bin_monotone
reason: Strict monotonicity of bin sizes is assumed via external_body constants — if any adjacent pair in the 73-entry table had a typo reversing the order, this would still verify due to trusted axioms, silently corrupting all bin-lookup reasoning

name: size_of_bin_outside_range_unconstrained
reason: `size_of_bin` is external_body with no constraining axiom outside indices 1-73, so the SMT solver could unify out-of-range values — if this verifies, any accidental out-of-bounds call returns an arbitrary but consistent value that could silently propagate

name: size_of_bin_divisible_by_intptr
reason: All bin sizes being divisible by 8 is asserted through trusted constants — if any constant were wrong (e.g., off by 1), `pfd_upper` would silently truncate via integer division, and this divisibility wouldn't catch it since it's also derived from the same unverified axiom

name: shift_div_pow2_zero_edge
reason: Shifting zero by the maximum valid shift (63) should yield 0 — while correct, `shift_is_div` only handles `shift < 64` and says nothing about shift == 64, so callers doing `x >> 64` would get no spec coverage and could silently use uninterpreted values
